reviewer: new-demo build wave (in-browser runnability proof first, then headless-Chrome real download+generation on index chat + all-ladder mobile+desktop overflow matrix, cached auto-init behaviour)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser generation verified BEFORE building: a Transformers.js@3.7.5 harness (device:'wasm', dtype:'q8') on HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct produced coherent, non-degenerate output ('Give three tips for staying focused' -> 'Set Clear Goals: Before starting your workday, define what you want to achieve…'). Headless build verify of the index page: fresh profile -> Download (~140 MB) -> ready -> Send 'Name three primary colors' streamed a real, coherent answer ('Red, Blue, and Green…') token-by-token, 0 console errors. The model is small so it can get facts wrong — the page discloses this honestly and never fakes a canned reply.
rubric.notes: Model HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct (Apache-2.0), llama-architecture decoder, ChatML template; the q8 build (onnx/model_quantized.onnx) runs on the universal WASM/CPU path. WebGPU is an optional accelerator with a WASM fallback.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity: the pipeline streams coherent continuation tokens conditioned on the prompt (verified on index chat, basics chat, practical rewriter, and wild greedy-vs-hot). The 'See inside' panel runs one real forward pass and shows the actual top-k next-token distribution (softmaxed last-position logits) — real probabilities, not a mock-up.
rubric.notes: text-generation task template ('streams coherent continuation/answer tokens conditioned on the prompt') fits.

rubric.evidence: conformance declares-model-id=pass (HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct linked to its card), declares-task=pass (text-generation), declares-backend=pass (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker + worker.js). At-a-glance names the exact model, architecture (llama, ~135M, 30 layers), dtype (q8), backend, and download size — no invented API.
rubric.notes: q8 on WASM; distinct checkpoint from the built 360M sibling (smollm2-chat).

rubric.evidence: conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass — every page loads through createModelLoader (backed by lib/model-cache.js, which scans Cache Storage for the modelId). A valid cached copy auto-inits with a checking->initialising->ready status and no Load button; first visit shows the honest Download (~140 MB) state and never auto-downloads. Per-model clear-cache control present.
rubric.notes: No hand-rolled Load button — identical shared architecture to the other Transformers.js LLM demos.

rubric.evidence: conformance loads-clean=pass (0 console errors on load across index + 4 ladder pages, verified headless), live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. Download progress streams into the loader progress bar; generation failures surface in the .status.err line with Retry via the loader. A Stop button interrupts generation (InterruptableStoppingCriteria).
rubric.notes: runs-without-webgpu-fallback: requiresWebGPU:false — the q8 WASM/CPU path runs everywhere, verified on the no-GPU headless runner; WebGPU is an optional accelerator.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised headless and is labelled + keyboard-operable: system-prompt textarea, message composer (Enter to send), Send/Stop/Clear buttons, example-prompt chips, temperature + top_p sliders, max-tokens number input, greedy checkbox (disables sampling sliders), and the tone <select> (practical) + hot-temperature slider (wild). buttons-have-names=pass.
rubric.notes: Sampling controls are wired to the real generation opts; the wild page runs the same prompt greedy vs sampled off-thread.

rubric.evidence: All streamed tokens, the readout (tok/s, first-token, tokens, backend), and the top-k bars come from the on-device Transformers.js run — no canned values. Distinct outputs per prompt/temperature and the honest 'the model can get facts wrong' framing prove the output is the real model, not a placeholder.

conformance buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass. The transcript uses aria-live=polite; status uses role=status aria-live=polite; the message textarea has a visually-hidden label; sliders/inputs are labelled; example chips are real buttons. Focus returns to the input after each turn.
rubric.notes: Reduced-motion honoured on the streaming caret (@media prefers-reduced-motion).

rubric.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Measured mobile (360x740 DPR3) horizontal overflow = 0 px on index + all 4 ladder pages, and desktop (1280x800) = 0 px, 0 console errors on each. The chat, split (greedy|hot), and RAG layouts use flex/grid with min-inline-size:0 and wrap on narrow viewports; bubbles use overflow-wrap:anywhere. Dark + light both legible.
rubric.notes: Composer buttons are >=44px tall; no 100vh traps.

rubric.evidence: All generation runs in a module Web Worker (new Worker) streaming tokens back incrementally, so the main thread only appends text — no blocking generation loop, INP stays low. The 'See inside' forward pass also runs in the worker.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: identify-inp-causes / break-up-long-tasks (inference off the main thread, token streaming, no long main-thread task).

guidanceConsulted.query: stream LLM tokens without INP regressions keep chat UI responsive web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run inference off the main thread and stream results incrementally; avoid long main-thread tasks so interaction stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — generation and the top-k forward pass run in a module worker; tokens stream back via postMessage and the main thread only appends escaped text to the transcript. No long main-thread task during generation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive chat composer and two-column split no horizontal overflow on mobile flexbox grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex/grid with min-inline-size:0 on items with wide content, gap + wrap, and overflow-wrap:anywhere on long text so nothing overflows horizontally.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the chat, greedy|hot split, and RAG panels use flex/grid with min-inline-size:0 and wrap; bubbles/answers use overflow-wrap:anywhere. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360x740 AND 1280x800 on all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible streaming chat live region status message labels keyboard
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce streaming output with aria-live, use role=status for progress, label all controls, and keep keyboard focus sensible.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — transcript + answer panes are aria-live=polite, status is role=status aria-live=polite, the composer textarea has a visually-hidden label, sliders/inputs/selects are labelled, and focus returns to the input after each turn.

openQuestions: A 135M model hallucinates facts readily; a follow-up could add a 'confidence hint' or steer example prompts toward transformation tasks (rewrite/summarise) where it's strongest.
A WebGPU q4f16 path (like the 360M sibling) could speed generation on capable devices — worth a progressive-enhancement follow-up once verifiable on a GPU runner.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a 'tiny vs small' comparison page (SmolLM2-135M vs the built 360M sibling) so visitors can feel the quality/speed/size trade-off on the same prompt.
Add a structured-extraction ladder demo (JSON out from a short instruction) to show where a tiny model is reliable.

summary: New demo: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct — the SMALLEST SmolLM2 instruct model (~135M, Apache-2.0), a distinct checkpoint from the built 360M sibling — run in the browser via Transformers.js (q8, universal WASM/CPU path) with token streaming off the main thread, real sampling controls, and a live top-k next-token distribution. Framed honestly as the extreme-low-end on-device chat model: fast and private, but small enough to get facts wrong (disclosed). Index + 4 ladder pages (basics chat / practical rewriter / wild temperature-playground / multi-model MiniLM+SmolLM2 RAG), all verified with real in-browser generation, cached auto-init, clean console, and mobile+desktop parity (0px overflow both classes).
